title: Fix Flat DaVinci Resolve Exports with Color Management
thumbnailConcept: Before and after export frame with scopes visible
hook: Your grade looks good in the Color page, but the export looks washed out. By the end, you will know the Project Settings to check so you can get a cleaner delivery.
promise: The viewer can identify the Color Management mismatch before exporting.
viewerPayoff: By the end, you will know which Resolve settings to inspect so you can export cleaner footage.

scriptOutline.hook:
## Hook
In this video you will fix flat Resolve exports without rebuilding the whole grade.
scriptOutline.problemContext:
## Problem / Context
When the Color page looks correct but the final file looks flat, the project color pipeline is often inconsistent.
scriptOutline.promisedOutcome:
## Promised Outcome
By the end, you will know what to inspect before sending the timeline to the Deliver page.
scriptOutline.steps:
## Steps
1. Open Project Settings.
2. Check Color Management.
3. Compare a short export from the Deliver page.
scriptOutline.proof:
## Demonstration / Proof
Show a before and after export and point to the corrected setting.
scriptOutline.recap:
## Recap / Takeaway
The takeaway is to check the project-level color pipeline before changing every node.
scriptOutline.cta:
## Call To Action
Comment with your camera format and watch the next Resolve tutorial on CST nodes.

script: Use a real project with a timeline, a visible node tree, and a short export comparison. Keep the language focused on the viewer's result and show the setting change on screen.
notes: Package exported by Episode Factory for Creator QA.
sourceNotes: Project Settings and Color Management wording checked manually before publishing.
checklist: Thumbnail reinforces the same export promise.
nextAction: Run Creator QA and fix any blockers before publishing.
